req.title: Fraud alert response (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests: Fraud Alert Confirmation & Auto-Freeze

```python
"""
Test suite for fraud alert acknowledgment and auto-freeze requirements.

Requirement:
  A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours;
  unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account.
  Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from freezegun import freeze_time

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production module imports (adjust to your actual package structure)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from fraud.alerts import FraudAlert, AlertStatus
from fraud.accounts import Account, AccountStatus
from fraud.service import FraudAlertService, AlertAcknowledgmentError


# ===========================================================================
# Constants & shared fixtures
# ===========================================================================

ALERT_WINDOW_HOURS = 24          # business requirement constant
FREEZE_DEADLINE_SECONDS = ALERT_WINDOW_HOURS * 3600   # 86 400 s

CUSTOMER_ID = "CUST-001"
ACCOUNT_ID  = "ACC-001"
ALERT_ID    = "ALERT-001"

BASE_TIME   = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
DEADLINE    = BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=ALERT_WINDOW_HOURS)   # exact boundary


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture()
def mock_account_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.get_by_id.return_value = Account(
        account_id=ACCOUNT_ID,
        customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
        status=AccountStatus.ACTIVE,
    )
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_alert_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_notification_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_audit_log():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture()
def service(mock_account_repo, mock_alert_repo,
            mock_notification_service, mock_audit_log):
    return FraudAlertService(
        account_repo=mock_account_repo,
        alert_repo=mock_alert_repo,
        notification_service=mock_notification_service,
        audit_log=mock_audit_log,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def open_alert():
    """An unacknowledged alert created at BASE_TIME."""
    return FraudAlert(
        alert_id=ALERT_ID,
        account_id=ACCOUNT_ID,
        customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
        status=AlertStatus.PENDING,
        created_at=BASE_TIME,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# Helper
# ===========================================================================

def make_alert(created_at: datetime,
               status: AlertStatus = AlertStatus.PENDING) -> FraudAlert:
    return FraudAlert(
        alert_id=ALERT_ID,
        account_id=ACCOUNT_ID,
        customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
        status=status,
        created_at=created_at,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Alert creation
# ===========================================================================

class TestFraudAlertCreation:

    def test_alert_created_with_pending_status(self, service, mock_alert_repo):
        """New alert must start in PENDING state."""
        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME):
            alert = service.raise_fraud_alert(ACCOUNT_ID, reason="Unusual transaction")

        assert alert.status == AlertStatus.PENDING

    def test_alert_stores_creation_timestamp(self, service):
        """Created-at timestamp must be recorded accurately."""
        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME):
            alert = service.raise_fraud_alert(ACCOUNT_ID, reason="Card cloning")

        assert alert.created_at == BASE_TIME

    def test_alert_creation_notifies_customer(self, service, mock_notification_service):
        """Customer must be notified immediately upon alert creation."""
        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME):
            service.raise_fraud_alert(ACCOUNT_ID, reason="Overseas transaction")

        mock_notification_service.send_fraud_alert.assert_called_once()

    def test_alert_creation_is_audit_logged(self, service, mock_audit_log):
        """Alert creation must be written to the audit log."""
        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME):
            service.raise_fraud_alert(ACCOUNT_ID, reason="test")

        mock_audit_log.record.assert_called_once()
        call_kwargs = mock_audit_log.record.call_args[1]
        assert call_kwargs.get("event") == "FRAUD_ALERT_RAISED"

    def test_duplicate_alert_raises_exception(self, service, mock_alert_repo):
        """A second open alert on the same account must be rejected."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_open_alert_for_account.return_value = make_alert(BASE_TIME)

        with pytest.raises(AlertAcknowledgmentError, match="open alert already exists"):
            service.raise_fraud_alert(ACCOUNT_ID, reason="duplicate")


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Acknowledgment – confirmation (CONFIRMED)
# ===========================================================================

class TestAlertConfirmation:

    def test_confirm_within_window_resolves_alert(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, open_alert):
        """Confirming fraud within 24 h marks alert CONFIRMED."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=1)):
            updated = service.acknowledge_alert(
                ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=True
            )

        assert updated.status == AlertStatus.CONFIRMED

    def test_confirm_keeps_account_active_temporarily(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, mock_account_repo, open_alert):
        """
        Confirming fraud within the window should NOT immediately freeze
        the account – that is a separate downstream workflow.
        """
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(minutes=30)):
            service.acknowledge_alert(ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=True)

        account = mock_account_repo.get_by_id.return_value
        # Freeze triggered by separate process, not this call
        assert account.status != AccountStatus.FROZEN

    def test_confirm_records_acknowledgment_timestamp(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, open_alert):
        """Acknowledged-at timestamp must be stored when confirming."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert
        ack_time = BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=10)

        with freeze_time(ack_time):
            updated = service.acknowledge_alert(
                ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=True
            )

        assert updated.acknowledged_at == ack_time


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Acknowledgment – denial (DISMISSED)
# ===========================================================================

class TestAlertDismissal:

    def test_deny_within_window_dismisses_alert(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, open_alert):
        """Denying fraud within 24 h marks alert DISMISSED."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=1)):
            updated = service.acknowledge_alert(
                ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=False
            )

        assert updated.status == AlertStatus.DISMISSED

    def test_deny_does_not_freeze_account(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, mock_account_repo, open_alert):
        """Denying fraud must leave the account ACTIVE."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=5)):
            service.acknowledge_alert(ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=False)

        account = mock_account_repo.get_by_id.return_value
        assert account.status == AccountStatus.ACTIVE


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Boundary conditions at exact 24-hour threshold
# ===========================================================================

class TestExactBoundaryConditions:
    """
    Requirements boundary: customer window = [0 s, 86400 s].
    - At exactly T+0 s  : alert just created, still open        → valid to ack
    - At exactly T+86400s: deadline moment                       → policy-defined (see tests)
    - At T+86400s + 1 s : one second past deadline              → too late
    """

    # ---- lower boundary (T = 0) ----------------------------------------

    def test_acknowledge_at_exact_creation_time(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, open_alert):
        """Acknowledging at the exact moment of creation is valid."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME):   # 0 seconds elapsed
            updated = service.acknowledge_alert(
                ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=False
            )

        assert updated.status == AlertStatus.DISMISSED

    # ---- one second before deadline ------------------------------------

    def test_acknowledge_one_second_before_deadline_is_valid(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, open_alert):
        """T + 86 399 s: acknowledgment must still be accepted."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert

        ack_time = BASE_TIME + timedelta(seconds=FREEZE_DEADLINE_SECONDS - 1)
        with freeze_time(ack_time):
            updated = service.acknowledge_alert(
                ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=False
            )

        assert updated.status == AlertStatus.DISMISSED

    # ---- exact deadline (inclusive boundary) ---------------------------

    def test_acknowledge_at_exact_deadline_is_accepted(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, open_alert):
        """
        T + 86 400 s exactly: the customer has used their full 24 hours.
        The requirement states 'within 24 hours'; the exact boundary is
        treated as the last valid moment (inclusive upper bound).
        """
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert

        with freeze_time(DEADLINE):   # exactly +86400 s
            updated = service.acknowledge_alert(
                ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=False
            )

        assert updated.status == AlertStatus.DISMISSED

    # ---- one second past deadline --------------------------------------

    def test_acknowledge_one_second_past_deadline_raises_error(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, open_alert):
        """T + 86 401 s: acknowledgment must be rejected; window has closed."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert

        expired_time = BASE_TIME + timedelta(seconds=FREEZE_DEADLINE_SECONDS + 1)
        with freeze_time(expired_time):
            with pytest.raises(AlertAcknowledgmentError, match="acknowledgment window has expired"):
                service.acknowledge_alert(
                    ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=False
                )

    # ---- one minute past deadline --------------------------------------

    def test_acknowledge_one_minute_past_deadline_raises_error(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, open_alert):
        """T + 86 460 s: clearly expired – same class of error."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert

        expired_time = BASE_TIME + timedelta(seconds=FREEZE_DEADLINE_SECONDS + 60)
        with freeze_time(expired_time):
            with pytest.raises(AlertAcknowledgmentError):
                service.acknowledge_alert(
                    ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=True
                )

    # ---- hours-level boundary checks -----------------------------------

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("hours_elapsed,should_succeed", [
        (0,    True),    # creation instant
        (1,    True),    # well within
        (12,   True),    # mid-point
        (23,   True),    # one hour to go
        (23.999, True),  # 3.6 s before deadline (floating-point hours)
        (24,   True),    # exact deadline – inclusive
        (24.001, False), # 3.6 s over – expired
        (25,   False),   # clearly over
        (48,   False),   # two days later
    ])
    def test_acknowledgment_boundary_parametrized(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, hours_elapsed, should_succeed):
        """Parametrized sweep across the 24-hour boundary."""
        alert = make_alert(created_at=BASE_TIME)
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = alert

        elapsed = timedelta(hours=hours_elapsed)
        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + elapsed):
            if should_succeed:
                updated = service.acknowledge_alert(
                    ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=False
                )
                assert updated.status in (AlertStatus.CONFIRMED, AlertStatus.DISMISSED)
            else:
                with pytest.raises(AlertAcknowledgmentError):
                    service.acknowledge_alert(
                        ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=False
                    )


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Auto-freeze on expiry
# ===========================================================================

class TestAutoFreezeOnExpiry:

    def test_expired_alert_triggers_account_freeze(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, mock_account_repo):
        """Unacknowledged alert past 24 h must cause account to be FROZEN."""
        alert = make_alert(created_at=BASE_TIME)
        mock_alert_repo.get_pending_alerts.return_value = [alert]

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=24, seconds=1)):
            service.process_expired_alerts()

        account = mock_account_repo.get_by_id.return_value
        mock_account_repo.save.assert_called_once()
        saved_account: Account = mock_account_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved_account.status == AccountStatus.FROZEN

    def test_expired_alert_status_set_to_auto_frozen(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo):
        """Alert status must become AUTO_FROZEN after the sweep job runs."""
        alert = make_alert(created_at=BASE_TIME)
        mock_alert_repo.get_pending_alerts.return_value = [alert]

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=25)):
            service.process_expired_alerts()

        mock_alert_repo.save.assert_called()
        saved_alert: FraudAlert = mock_alert_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved_alert.status == AlertStatus.AUTO_FROZEN

    def test_non_expired_alert_does_not_freeze_account(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, mock_account_repo):
        """Alert still within 24 h must NOT trigger an auto-freeze."""
        alert = make_alert(created_at=BASE_TIME)
        mock_alert_repo.get_pending_alerts.return_value = [alert]

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=23, minutes=59)):
            service.process_expired_alerts()

        # save should not be called with a FROZEN account
        for c in mock_account_repo.save.call_args_list:
            saved: Account = c[0][0]
            assert saved.status != AccountStatus.FROZEN

    def test_alert_at_exact_deadline_not_auto_frozen(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, mock_account_repo):
        """At exactly T + 86 400 s the alert is still valid; no freeze yet."""
        alert = make_alert(created_at=BASE_TIME)
        mock_alert_repo.get_pending_alerts.return_value = [alert]

        with freeze_time(DEADLINE):
            service.process_expired_alerts()

        for c in mock_account_repo.save.call_args_list:
            saved: Account = c[0][0]
            assert saved.status != AccountStatus.FROZEN

    def test_alert_one_second_past_deadline_is_auto_frozen(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, mock_account_repo):
        """T + 86 401 s: the window is closed; account must be frozen."""
        alert = make_alert(created_at=BASE_TIME)
        mock_alert_repo.get_pending_alerts.return_value = [alert]

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(seconds=FREEZE_DEADLINE_SECONDS + 1)):
            service.process_expired_alerts()

        mock_account_repo.save.assert_called()
        saved: Account = mock_account_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved.status == AccountStatus.FROZEN

    def test_auto_freeze_is_audit_logged(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, mock_audit_log):
        """Auto-freeze events must be written to the audit log."""
        alert = make_alert(created_at=BASE_TIME)
        mock_alert_repo.get_pending_alerts.return_value = [alert]

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=25)):
            service.process_expired_alerts()

        logged_events = [
            c[1].get("event") for c in mock_audit_log.record.call_args_list
        ]
        assert "ACCOUNT_AUTO_FROZEN" in logged_events

    def test_auto_freeze_notifies_customer(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, mock_notification_service):
        """Customer must receive a notification when their account is auto-frozen."""
        alert = make_alert(created_at=BASE_TIME)
        mock_alert_repo.get_pending_alerts.return_value = [alert]

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=25)):
            service.process_expired_alerts()

        mock_notification_service.send_freeze_notification.assert_called_once_with(
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID
        )

    def test_multiple_expired_alerts_freeze_respective_accounts(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, mock_account_repo):
        """Batch job must freeze every account with an expired alert."""
        alerts = [
            make_alert(created_at=BASE_TIME - timedelta(hours=i))
            for i in range(3)
        ]
        for a in alerts:
            a.alert_id = f"ALERT-{a.created_at.timestamp()}"
            a.account_id = f"ACC-{a.created_at.timestamp()}"

        mock_alert_repo.get_pending_alerts.return_value = alerts

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=25)):
            service.process_expired_alerts()

        assert mock_account_repo.save.call_count == len(alerts)

    def test_already_frozen_account_not_frozen_again(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, mock_account_repo):
        """Idempotency: running the job twice must not double-freeze or error."""
        alert = make_alert(created_at=BASE_TIME)
        mock_alert_repo.get_pending_alerts.return_value = [alert]

        frozen_account = Account(
            account_id=ACCOUNT_ID,
            customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
            status=AccountStatus.FROZEN,
        )
        mock_account_repo.get_by_id.return_value = frozen_account

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=25)):
            service.process_expired_alerts()   # must not raise

        # Confirm save was called but status remains FROZEN (not double-set)
        if mock_account_repo.save.called:
            saved: Account = mock_account_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
            assert saved.status == AccountStatus.FROZEN


# ===========================================================================
# 6. Guard rails – wrong customer / tampered requests
# ===========================================================================

class TestSecurityGuardRails:

    def test_wrong_customer_cannot_acknowledge_alert(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo, open_alert):
        """A different customer_id must not be able to acknowledge the alert."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_alert

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=1)):
            with pytest.raises(AlertAcknowledgmentError, match="customer mismatch"):
                service.acknowledge_alert(
                    ALERT_ID, customer_id="CUST-EVIL", is_fraud=False
                )

    def test_already_acknowledged_alert_cannot_be_re_acknowledged(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo):
        """A CONFIRMED alert must reject a second acknowledgment."""
        confirmed_alert = make_alert(BASE_TIME, status=AlertStatus.CONFIRMED)
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = confirmed_alert

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=1)):
            with pytest.raises(AlertAcknowledgmentError, match="already acknowledged"):
                service.acknowledge_alert(
                    ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=True
                )

    def test_nonexistent_alert_raises_error(
            self, service, mock_alert_repo):
        """Acknowledging a non-existent alert ID must raise a clear error."""
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = None

        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=1)):
            with pytest.raises(AlertAcknowledgmentError, match="alert not found"):
                service.acknowledge_alert(
                    "DOES-NOT-EXIST", customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=False
                )


# ===========================================================================
# 7. Timezone awareness
# ===========================================================================

class TestTimezoneHandling:

    def test_deadline_is_calculated_in_utc(self, service, mock_alert_repo):
        """Deadline calculation must use UTC; local-time offsets must not shift it."""
        # Alert created at midnight UTC
        utc_midnight = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 0, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
        alert = make_alert(created_at=utc_midnight)
        mock_alert_repo.get_by_id.return_value = alert

        # Try to acknowledge at 23:59:59 UTC on the following day (still valid)
        just_before = utc_midnight + timedelta(hours=24) - timedelta(seconds=1)
        with freeze_time(just_before):
            updated = service.acknowledge_alert(
                ALERT_ID, customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID, is_fraud=False
            )

        assert updated.status == AlertStatus.DISMISSED

    def test_naive_datetime_creation_is_rejected(self, service):
        """Fraud alerts created with timezone-naive datetimes must raise ValueError."""
        naive_dt = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0)   # no tzinfo

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="timezone-aware"):
            FraudAlert(
                alert_id=ALERT_ID,
                account_id=ACCOUNT_ID,
                customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
                status=AlertStatus.PENDING,
                created_at=naive_dt,
            )
```

---

## Design Decisions & Coverage Rationale

| Category | What is tested | Why |
|---|---|---|
| **Alert lifecycle** | `PENDING → CONFIRMED / DISMISSED / AUTO_FROZEN` | Validates all valid state transitions |
| **Exact lower bound** | Acknowledge at T+0 s | Ensures no off-by-one at creation |
| **Exact upper bound** | Acknowledge at T+86 400 s (inclusive) | Validates the "within 24 hours" contract is treated inclusively |
| **One-second over** | T+86 401 s rejected | Classic boundary-value analysis |
| **Parametrized sweep** | 9 data points across the window | Broad but efficient coverage of the continuum |
| **Auto-freeze trigger** | Past deadline → `AccountStatus.FROZEN` | Core safety requirement |
| **Idempotency** | Already-frozen account not re-frozen | Prevents double-processing bugs |
| **Security guards** | Wrong customer, re-acknowledgment, missing alert | Prevents fraud in the fraud-detection system itself |
| **Timezone** | UTC enforcement; naive datetime rejection | Time-calculation bugs are the #1 cause of deadline drift |
| **Audit & notification** | Both are asserted on creation and freeze | Non-functional but legally required side-effects |

# Pytest Tests: Fraud Alert Confirmation & Auto-Freeze

```python
"""
Test suite for fraud alert acknowledgment and auto-freeze requirements.

Requirement:
  A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours;
  unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account.
  Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from freezegun import freeze_time

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Production module imports (adjust to your actual package structure)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from fraud.alerts import FraudAlert, AlertStatus
from fraud.accounts import Account, AccountStatus
from fraud.service import FraudAlertService, AlertAcknowledgmentError


# ===========================================================================
# Constants & shared fixtures
# ===========================================================================

ALERT_WINDOW_HOURS = 24          # business requirement constant
FREEZE_DEADLINE_SECONDS = ALERT_WINDOW_HOURS * 3600   # 86 400 s

CUSTOMER_ID = "CUST-001"
ACCOUNT_ID  = "ACC-001"
ALERT_ID    = "ALERT-001"

BASE_TIME   = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0, 0, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
DEADLINE    = BASE_TIME + timedelta(hours=ALERT_WINDOW_HOURS)   # exact boundary


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture()
def mock_account_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.get_by_id.return_value = Account(
        account_id=ACCOUNT_ID,
        customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,
        status=AccountStatus.ACTIVE,
    )
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_alert_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_notification_service():
    return MagicMock()

def make_alert(created_at: datetime,
               status: AlertStatus = AlertStatus.PENDING) -> FraudAlert:

        customer_id=CUSTOMER_ID,

        created_at=created_at,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Alert creation
# ===========================================================================

class TestFraudAlertCreation:

    def test_alert_created_with_pending_status(self, service, mock_alert_repo):
        """New alert must start in PENDING state."""
        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME):
            alert = service.raise_fraud_alert(ACCOUNT_ID, reason="Unusual transaction")

        assert alert.status == AlertStatus.PENDING

    def test_alert_stores_creation_timestamp(self, service):
        """Created-at timestamp must be recorded accurately."""
        with freeze_time(BASE_TIME):
            alert = service.raise_fraud_alert(ACCOUNT_ID, reason="Card cloning")

        assert alert.created_at == BASE_TIME
